mission.title: harbor: New cosign 2.6 signature format is not recognized by Harbor
mission.description: New cosign 2.6 signature format is not recognized by Harbor. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current harbor deployment
steps.description:
Verify your harbor version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
helm list -n harbor 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working harbor installation.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
With cosign 2.6, with `--new-bundle-format` tag, the format of the signature by cosign has changed.

steps.title: Apply the cosign signature format workaround
steps.description:
Configure cosign to use a signature format compatible with your Harbor version:
```bash
# Sign with legacy tag-based format (compatible with older Harbor)
COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL=0 cosign sign --key cosign.key <harbor-host>/<project>/<image>:<tag>

# Or explicitly set the attachment format
cosign sign --attachment-tag-prefix=sha256- --key cosign.key <harbor-host>/<project>/<image>:<tag>
```sql
Alternatively, upgrade Harbor to a version that supports the new cosign 2.6+ OCI referrers API format.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "New cosign 2.6 signature format is not recognized by Harbor" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Cosign 2.6 introduced a new signature format that Harbor does not recognize, causing signature verification to fail. The issue is that newer cosign versions changed the default signature format. The workaround is to use cosign with the --registry-referrers-mode=oci-1-1 flag disabled, or to configure cosign to use the legacy tag-based signature attachment format that Harbor supports.
codeSnippets:
# Sign with legacy tag-based format (compatible with older Harbor)
COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL=0 cosign sign --key cosign.key <harbor-host>/<project>/<image>:<tag>

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
